mechanism_narrative: Falling federal funds rates compress equity discount factors and lower corporate financing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates exceeding 6.0 percent signal severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending, forcing equity markets to price in recessionary earnings declines.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts confirm a broad-based consumer demand contraction, triggering negative equity returns as corporate revenues fall.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly drops in industrial production expose immediate supply chain disruptions and inventory shortages, causing short-term market panic and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of cases following industrial production declines exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation rates surpassing the federal funds rate by more than 2 percentage points create negative real yields, eroding household purchasing power and prompting aggressive central bank tightening that depresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Periods of negative real rates consistently yield positive 60-day SPX returns, contradicting the valuation compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, driving positive equity returns through improved earnings expectations.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent indicates economic stagnation and weakening corporate profit margins, triggering negative forward equity returns as investors discount future cash flows.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >70% of quarters where GDP growth remains below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling employment signals automated productivity gains that expand corporate profit margins, driving short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in walk-forward testing.
